title: On the Symmetries of Anisotropic Spin Interaction Models
abstract: We show that anisotropic spin interactions do not merely break spin-space group (SSG) symmetries, but instead twist them through cohomology invariants, yielding symmetry classes beyond subgroups of $O(3)\times \operatorname{Isom}(\mathbb{R}^3) $. This requires redefining the spin-only group $S_0$ in terms of proper spin rotations. Based on this unitary $S_0$, we formulate a twisted SSG (tSSG) theory that captures the complete set of spin-space symmetries. We then study a spin-1 model with tSSG symmetry using linear flavor wave theory and find topological quadrupolar excitations defined on a spin Brillouin Klein-bottle rather than the conventional torus. Specifically, the bosonic BdG Hamiltonian satisfies a glide reflection sewing relation, the ribbon spectrum exhibits Möbius boundary states. These topological excitations are classified by $ \mathbb{Z}_2 $, enforced by the nonorientability of the Klein-bottle.
summary: This paper proposes a new theoretical framework called twisted Spin-Space Groups to accurately describe the symmetries of anisotropic magnetic systems. It reveals that these 'twisted' symmetries can create exotic topological excitations on non-orientable manifolds like the Klein bottle.
main_problem: The paper addresses the inadequacy of conventional spin-space group (SSG) frameworks in describing anisotropic spin interactions, which do not merely break symmetries but 'twist' them through cohomology invariants.
main_result: The authors develop a twisted Spin-Space Group (tSSG) theory and demonstrate that this leads to topological quadrupolar excitations on a spin Brillouin Klein-bottle, characterized by a Z2 invariant and Mobius boundary states.
method: The study utilizes group extension theory and cohomology invariants to classify symmetries, combined with linear flavor wave theory and Bogoliubov-de Gennes formalism to analyze the bosonic spectrum.
model_or_system: A two-dimensional spin-1 model on a square lattice featuring anisotropic exchange interactions (including Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya and XY components) and single-ion anisotropy.
key_observables: Bosonic excitation branches, edge mode energies, decay factor, and a Z2 topological invariant.
important_parameters: Single-ion anisotropy (D), exchange anisotropy parameters (a, b, c, lambda, alpha, beta, gamma), and the large-D ferroquadrupolar limit.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the lattice symmetry group contains a C4 cyclic subgroup and focuses on the large-D limit for the ground state vacuum.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the Hamiltonian components, including the AFM background field, and visualizes the site-dependent spin operations associated with C4 rotation and time-reversal symmetry.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of symmetry breaking vs. twisting, develops the mathematical framework for tSSG using group cohomology, applies this to a specific spin-1 model, and concludes with the topological implications for excitations.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Spin model with twisted collinear SSG symmetries eC4 and eT . Panel (a) illustrates the Hamiltonian and the AFM background field (teal), while panel (b) shows the site- dependent spin operations associated with C4 (red) and T (purple), respectively. Here, each θ ∈[0, 2π) in the panel (b) stands for a local spin rotation Rz(θ) ∈SO(2)z.

FIG. 2. The Hamiltonian (12) realizing tSSGs with arbitrary S0. The upper layer represents the spin-exchange Hamilto- nian Hbond, while the lower layer represents the on-site single- ion anisotropy Hamiltonian Hsite.
